text.title: cost-estimation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cost-estimation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM outputs suspiciously round numbers ($50k, $100k, $200k) without showing the calculation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Round numbers are anchors from training data, not estimates; require the bottom-up calculation or reject the output.

failure-mode.description: Using a single overhead multiplier (1.3x) globally across all regions and employment types.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overhead loading varies by region, tax regime, and benefits structure (1.3x to 2.0x); a single multiplier is wrong for cross-border projects.

failure-mode.description: Including both risk-driven contingency AND a flat 15% on top of it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Double-charging inflates the budget and misrepresents the risk exposure; pick one method and document the choice.

failure-mode.description: Re-estimating after work has started without accounting for sunk costs, anchoring on the original number.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Re-estimates must derive from remaining scope only; anchoring to the original produces estimates that are always "a little over" the baseline.
